label: 492_municipal_municipal bond_municipal bonds_bond
description: The U.S. municipal bond market faced concerns over the health of state and local governments during the post-pandemic economic recovery, as defaults threatened the safety of these traditionally safe investments. Structural budget problems were exacerbated by the recession, leading to ongoing risks for municipalities and their bond issuers.

newest_articles.title: The “Privatization” of Municipal Debt
newest_articles.excerpt: We study the determinants of local governments’ reliance on bank loans using granular data from the Federal Reserve. Governments that are larger, rely on stable revenue sources, or have higher spending relative to revenues are more likely to borrow from banks. central_articles.excerpt: As the U.S. economy remains weakened by the Covid-19 pandemic, concern persists for the health and resilience of the municipal bond market.
